faq.sub: The most important questions about SOVEREIGN 2030 — transparent, precise, without marketing language.

0.a: SOVEREIGN 2030 is an autonomous Life-OS — a digital twin that analyzes your contracts, monitors your finances, and acts autonomously in your interest. Unlike conventional finance apps, SOVEREIGN is built on a Boundary-First architecture: you define the rules, the AI executes — fully transparent, fully traceable.

1.q: How does SOVEREIGN protect my personal data?
1.a: SOVEREIGN uses a 4-layer architecture: AgentMemory (isolated data storage without hyperscaler dependency), Privacy Guardian (validates every API call against your consent rules before execution), Execution Center (HITL/HOTL — you decide when the AI acts autonomously), and a full Audit Trail. All data is processed exclusively in EU data centers. SOVEREIGN is fully GDPR-compliant and EU AI Act-compliant.

2.a: Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) means you are explicitly asked before every critical AI action. With Human-on-the-Loop (HOTL), the AI acts autonomously within your predefined boundaries but informs you of every action in the Audit Trail. You decide for each category of actions which mode you prefer — maximum control or maximum automation.

3.q: Can SOVEREIGN automatically cancel my contracts?
3.a: Yes — in HOTL mode, SOVEREIGN can automatically cancel contracts when you've defined corresponding rules (e.g., 'Cancel all subscriptions over €15/month that I haven't used in the last 30 days'). In HITL mode, SOVEREIGN prepares cancellation letters and presents them to you for approval. Every cancellation is tamper-proof documented in the Audit Trail.

4.q: Is SOVEREIGN GDPR and EU AI Act compliant?
4.a: Yes, fully. SOVEREIGN was designed from the ground up for EU compliance: all data in EU data centers (Google Cloud, region europe-west3), full data deletion on request, purpose limitation through Privacy Guardian, transparent AI decisions through Audit Trail. The EU AI Act classifies SOVEREIGN as a Low-Risk AI System with full transparency compliance.

5.a: SOVEREIGN offers three tiers: FREE (free, basic contract analysis), PRO (€6.99/month — full automation, Finance Guardian, Brainstormer AI) and SHIELD (€14.99/month — maximum protection for family & business, incl. PSD2 banking, Family Vault, Priority Support). No hidden costs, no commissions, no data sharing with third parties.

6.q: How is SOVEREIGN different from Check24 or Finanzguru?
6.a: The fundamental difference: Check24 and Finanzguru are funded by commissions for contract closings — their AI optimizes for their revenue, not your interest. SOVEREIGN is funded exclusively by subscription fees. No incentive for contract recommendations. Additionally: SOVEREIGN has a full Audit Trail, Boundary-First architecture and Human-in-the-Loop control — none of these platforms offer that.

7.q: What is a digital twin in SOVEREIGN's context?
7.a: The SOVEREIGN Twin is an AI instance fully trained on your personal data (contracts, finances, preferences) that acts on your behalf. Unlike generic AI assistants, your twin has no access to external data — it operates exclusively within your data boundaries. This makes it more precise, safer, and 100% tailored to your situation.

8.q: Which contract types can SOVEREIGN analyze?
8.a: SOVEREIGN analyzes all common contract types: mobile phone contracts, electricity & gas contracts, insurance (auto, liability, household), rental agreements, software subscriptions (Netflix, Spotify etc.), financial products (loans, deposits), telecommunications contracts and employment contracts. Analysis includes deadlines, cancellation rights, hidden clauses, price increase rights and risk assessment.

9.q: How secure is my bank connection with SOVEREIGN?
9.a: Banking integration is done exclusively via the regulated PSD2 interface via finAPI — the same standard banks themselves use. SOVEREIGN stores no bank data, only aggregated transaction data. finAPI is BaFin-regulated and ISO 27001-certified. The Privacy Guardian blocks any data access not explicitly approved by you.

hub.sub: Comprehensive guides on contract law, data protection and autonomous AI — reviewed by lawyers, read by the community.

0.title: Cancel Contract: The Ultimate Guide 2026
0.desc: BGB §314, §628, special termination rights, deadlines and free templates. Everything you need — on one page.

1.title: Object to Price Increase: Your Rights
1.desc: Netflix, electricity, telecom — how to legally object to price increases. With template letter.

2.title: Digital Sovereignty 2030: Why It Matters Now
2.desc: What digital sovereignty means, why it becomes the defining topic in 2026, and how you as an individual can regain control of your digital life.

3.title: GDPR Data Request: How to Get Your Data Back

4.title: Subscription Trap: Recognize, Challenge, Escape
4.desc: Hidden subscriptions cost Germans an average of €340/year. How to end the subscription trap permanently.

5.title: EU AI Act Explained: What You Need to Know in 2026

6.title: 5 EU Laws That Change Your Digital Life
6.desc: GDPR, DSA, DMA, EU AI Act and Data Act — complete compliance overview with penalty risks and checklists.

boundary.title: Boundary Conditions

boundary.autoApproveDesc: Allows the system to automatically execute contract switches and optimisations below this threshold without asking.
boundary.privacyDesc: Anonymises sensitive financial data before context is sent to LLM providers (PII Redaction).
